headline: US blockade of Strait of Hormuz will last 'as long as it takes', says Defence Secretary
summary: Pete Hegseth urges Iran to 'choose wisely' as 13 ships have been forced to turn around since the blockade began on Monday.

headline: Satellite images reveal scale of Israeli demolitions as Lebanese villages destroyed
summary: BBC Verify analysis found more than 1,400 buildings had been destroyed since 2 March.

headline: South African opposition figure Julius Malema sentenced to five years in prison
summary: Malema is appealing against the decision following conviction for firing an assault rifle in public during a 2018 rally.

headline: Pope Leo criticises 'tyrants' who spend billions on wars, days after Trump spat
summary: The Pope's unusually forceful remarks in Cameroon follow a high-profile spat with US President Donald Trump, who called the Pope weak on crime.

headline: Turkey sees its second school shooting in as many days
summary: A student killed 4 people on Wednesday, following another shooting that injured 16 at a high school on Tuesday. 162 people detained over online praise for attacks.

headline: Brazil's former spy chief released from ICE detention in Florida
summary: Bolsonaro ally Alexandre Ramagem was stopped by immigration agents in Orlando on Monday.

headline: India's government launches bid to overhaul democratic system
summary: Bills to increase size of parliament and reserve a third of seats for women; opposition dismisses it as attempt to manipulate elections.

headline: Burundi's communications minister dies in accident
summary: Gabby Bugaga died on Thursday in what the presidency described as an accident.

headline: Iran war has left Britain's armed forces exposed
summary: Pressure mounts on Prime Minister Keir Starmer to invest in defence after years of warnings about UK's shrinking capabilities.

headline: American YouTuber sentenced to 6 months in South Korean prison for offensive stunts
summary: Johnny Somali sentenced following offensive stunts including incidents at statue sites.
